name: Slides
description: Build a deck where every slide arranges other Lolly tools in a chosen layout, with its own title, background and speaker notes — then export to PowerPoint, PDF, GIF or MP4.

inputs.label: Deck size
inputs.help: One size for the whole deck — PowerPoint gives every slide the same size. Override it in pixels in the export bar.

inputs.label: Transition
inputs.help: How each slide arrives over the one before it. Only GIF, MP4 and WebM carry the motion — PowerPoint and PDF get every slide still.

inputs.label: Transition length
inputs.help: Seconds each slide takes to arrive. A fade takes the whole length; a slide or zoom glides over it while the opacity snaps in, which keeps GIF/MP4 exports crisp.

inputs.label: Seconds per slide
inputs.help: How long each slide holds before the next one arrives. This plus the transitions is the length of the exported clip.

inputs.label: Loop
inputs.help: How many times the deck plays in the GIF/MP4.

inputs.label: Pause on slide
inputs.help: 0 plays the whole deck in the preview. Set a slide number to hold it there while you edit — exports still play.

inputs.label: Slides
inputs.help: Each block is one slide. Pick its layout when you add it; in a slot, choose another Lolly tool or an image from the picker's Tools tab. Reorder by dragging.

fields.label: Slot 1 — tool or image
fields.help: Paste a Lolly tool link, or pick a tool in the picker's Tools tab, and its render fills the slot.

fields.label: Speaker notes
fields.placeholder: Presenter notes — never shown on the slide.
fields.help: Travels into PowerPoint's notes pane. Never rendered on the slide, in any format.

default.title: Slides
default.subtitle: Every slide can hold another Lolly tool.
default.notes: Open with why the deck exists — the agenda can wait.

default.title: One layout per slide
default.subtitle: Title, hero, split, columns, grid
default.notes: Pick the layout from the Add slide menu — it is fixed once the slide exists.

default.title: Drop a tool into a slot
default.subtitle: A QR code, a chart, a map — rendered straight into the slide.
default.notes: Slots read from the asset picker's Tools tab, so a slot stays live: change the tool link and the slide re-renders.

default.title: Export the deck
default.subtitle: PowerPoint · PDF · GIF · MP4
default.notes: PowerPoint and PDF get every slide still. GIF and MP4 get the transitions.
